facts.text: Manhasset sits atop the Harbor Hill Moraine, a terminal moraine formed by glaciers during the Wisconsin Glacial Episode.
The name Manhasset comes from a Native American word meaning “the island neighborhood.”
In 1898 the Long Island Railroad reached Manhasset, bringing wealthy New Yorkers who built country homes with easy access to Manhattan.
Manhasset’s historic American-style homes line Plandome Road, a stretch known for its distinctive local merchants and high‑end shops.
The Manhasset Valley School once served the children of Gold Coast estates and later became a key institution for the African‑American community before closing in the 1960s.
Manhasset’s Chamber of Commerce traces its roots back to the early 20th century, preserving the hamlet’s history and supporting local businesses.
